items.prompt: exposure: scripts/.discord_webhook.txt in Scheduler is git-tracked and contains a live discord.com/api/webhooks URL (first committed in cf05134, feat(scheduler): v0.2.0). Mitigate: (1) add `scripts/.discord_webhook.txt` to .gitignore; (2) `git rm --cached scripts/.discord_webhook.txt`; (3) commit the redaction; (4) rotate the webhook on Discord (the leaked URL is permanently in git history and trivially recoverable). History scrub via `git filter-repo --path scripts/.discord_webhook.txt --invert-paths` is a separate manual call after rotation. Compare YaC + YaApothecary, which already use `.discord_webhook.txt.example` + gitignored real file.
items.notes: Surfaced by working-tree-scan-weekly 2026-05-29. Same secret-file pattern as the YaB .finances/ exposure earlier in the cycle.
items.note: | 2026-06-04 verified STILL LIVE: scripts/.discord_webhook.txt is git-tracked, absent from .gitignore, contains live discord.com/api/webhooks URL. P0 blocks auto-fix (git rm --cached + commit + push + rotate on Discord = Nick action). Surfaced.

items.prompt: YaC has one stash entry: `stash@{0}: On main: scraper WIP`. Review and either apply (then ship or re-stash with clearer label), or drop. `git -C X:\YesAndChains stash show -p stash@{0}` to inspect; `git stash apply stash@{0}` or `git stash drop stash@{0}` to act. Stashes live in the reflog and can be reconstructed for 90 days, so the safer move is `apply` and decide in-tree, not blind drop.

Do NOT rewrite historical CONTEXT.md changelog entries that name these tools. | 2026-06-04 17:08Z drain: blocking dep yac-mnew-ship-0.34.2 now COMPLETED (0.34.2 shipped, archive move committed), so unblocked. NOT auto-drained: not a mechanical path swap. Stale line numbers (CONTEXT.md:210->235, BACKLOG.md:395/496/1051->248/398/499/1054). CONTEXT.md:235 names scrape_pipeline.py inside a LIVE scraper-workflow description while the tool now lives in tools/_archived/ and is absent from the CLAUDE.md 'six active scripts' list -- that's a live-vs-dead-workflow judgment, not a repoint. BACKLOG.md 248/398/499 + CONTEXT.md:9 are historical/forward-fix rows -> leave untouched per item spec. 218KB CONTEXT.md = FUSE risk; needs a focused session. Left pending. | 2026-06-05 13:10Z drain: grounded the remaining scope. CONTEXT.md refs are all historical EXCEPT line ~236 (the 'Course Data Gathering -- Sonnet' manual scraper-session description, which names scrape_pipeline.py as the live tool). That workflow was superseded by ADR 0021 (local cron via cron_course_refresh.py + run-course-refresh.ps1 / Windows Task Scheduler); CLAUDE.md's six-active-scripts list does not mention scrape_pipeline.py at all. BACKLOG 30.2 ('Update tools/scrape_pipeline.py to write to per-state shard') is likely obsolete for the same reason. So this is NOT a path repoint -- it is a confirm-and-retire of a dead manual workflow in a 336KB CONTEXT.md over FUSE. Recommend: Nick confirms the manual scraper session is retired, then rewrite CONTEXT.md:236 to describe the cron pipeline and mark BACKLOG 30.2 done/obsolete in a focused session. Left pending.
